sections: What a House Clearance Day Actually Involves

Knowing what to expect takes a great deal of the worry out of a clearance, especially when the property belonged to someone you have lost. On the day, our team arrives at the agreed time with a fully equipped van, protective coverings, tools and sturdy packing materials. We begin by walking through the property with you — or with the executor, landlord or agent — so we understand which rooms are in scope, which items are staying, and anything that needs particular care. Nothing is touched until we are clear on your wishes.

From there we work methodically, room by room, so progress is visible and the property never descends into chaos. As a family-run Sussex firm operating since 2016 from our base at Doryln House in Ashington, we have refined a calm, respectful rhythm to clearance work. We protect floors and doorways, lift heavy furniture safely, and keep walkways clear throughout. A typical home is cleared in a single day, though larger or fuller properties may run across two. Whatever the size, we leave the property swept through and ready for sale, letting or handover.

  • An initial walk-through to confirm scope and any items to retain
  • Careful, room-by-room sorting and removal
  • Floor and doorway protection to prevent damage
  • Safe lifting and dismantling of heavy or awkward furniture
  • A final tidy so the property is ready to hand over
Handling Probate & Bereavement Clearances with Care

Clearing the home of someone who has passed away is one of the hardest tasks a family faces, and it rarely happens at a convenient moment. We approach probate and bereavement clearances with patience and discretion, working entirely at your pace. There is never any pressure to make instant decisions, and we are happy to liaise with solicitors, executors or estate agents on your behalf so you have one less thing to manage during a difficult time.

We understand that an ordinary-looking item can carry enormous sentimental weight. Letters, photographs, jewellery, documents and small keepsakes are set aside carefully so they are never lost in the process. If you would rather not be present, we can keep you updated by phone and flag anything that looks personal or valuable before it goes anywhere. Our LAPADA membership reflects genuine experience in handling antiques and items of value, so where a piece may be worth something, we will tell you rather than let it disappear. That blend of sensitivity and expertise is why families, and professionals such as Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, recommend us for this kind of work.

Not every clearance follows a bereavement. Many of our customers are downsizing to a smaller home, clearing a property before putting it on the market, or decluttering ahead of a move. When you are moving to a more compact home, deciding what comes with you and what is left behind can feel overwhelming. We help you work through it gently, separating what fits your new life from what can move on, then dealing with the rest in one clean sweep.

Landlords and tenants also rely on us for end-of-tenancy clearances, where a property needs to be emptied of left-behind furniture and belongings before the next let. We work quickly and to deadline, returning the property to a lettable condition. Because we are a full removals firm as well as a clearance company, decluttering before a move flows naturally into the move itself — you can read more about how we run our house removals service if a relocation is part of your plans. Whatever the reason, the aim is the same: a clear, calm space and far less to think about.

  • Downsizing support, helping you choose what travels to a smaller home
  • Pre-sale declutters to present a property at its best
  • End-of-tenancy clearances completed to a landlord’s deadline
  • Garage, loft, shed and outbuilding clearances
Sorting Keep, Donate, Sell & Recycle Responsibly

A good clearance is not simply about emptying a property — it is about doing right by the belongings inside it. We sort items thoughtfully into what you wish to keep, what can be donated, what may have resale value, and what genuinely needs recycling or disposal. Wherever possible, usable furniture, clothing, kitchenware and household goods are directed to charity rather than landfill, so that things which still have life in them go on to help others.

For pieces with real value, our antiques knowledge as a LAPADA member means we can identify items worth selling rather than giving away, and advise you honestly on the best route. The remainder is separated for recycling and responsible disposal, keeping waste to a minimum. We take environmental responsibility seriously and always handle disposal through proper channels. The result is a clearance you can feel comfortable about — respectful of the belongings, kinder to the planet, and free of the guilt that comes with skips full of perfectly usable items.
Combining Clearance with a Move or Storage

One of the advantages of choosing a firm that does far more than clearances is that everything can be handled under one roof. If you are clearing one property and moving belongings into another, we can clear, pack and relocate in a single coordinated operation, saving you the hassle of juggling separate companies. Our full packing service means fragile and treasured items are wrapped properly before they travel, while smaller jobs can be handled by our flexible man and van service.

Sometimes you simply need breathing space — a place to keep the things you want to keep while a sale completes or a new home is found. Our secure storage facilities are ideal for exactly that, and where you only need a few weeks we also offer short-term storage with no long commitment. Belongings can move straight from the cleared property into store, then on to their final home when you are ready. We cover homes right across West Sussex as well as East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, so wherever the property sits, we can bring the whole job together seamlessly.
Why Families & Professionals Trust Wolves for Clearances

Choosing who to let into a loved one’s home, or your own, is a matter of trust. We are a Checkatrade-verified, fully insured business carrying £10m liability cover, so you are protected throughout the work. More than the paperwork, though, what sets us apart is the manner in which the work is done — calmly, honestly and without ever rushing you. Since 2016 we have built our reputation on treating every property and every family with the same respect we would want for our own.

That reputation is reflected in the professionals who recommend us, including the estate agencies Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, who trust us with their clients’ properties. Our LAPADA membership underlines the care we take with antiques and items of value. If you would like to talk something through before committing, we are always happy to listen — you can request a no-obligation quote or call the team directly on 01903 893731.
faqs: How do you quote for a house clearance?

Every property is different, so we prefer to quote on what is actually there rather than a one-size-fits-all price. Where possible we will arrange a brief look at the property, or talk you through it room by room over the phone, taking into account the volume of belongings, access, any heavy or awkward items, and whether storage or a move is involved. You then receive a clear, no-obligation price with no hidden extras. You can start the process by requesting a quote or calling us on 01903 893731.
Can you carry out a clearance sensitively after a bereavement?

Yes, and it is work we take particular care over. We move at your pace, never pressuring you to make quick decisions, and we set aside any letters, photographs, documents or keepsakes so nothing personal is lost. If you would rather not be present, we can keep you updated and flag anything that looks valuable or sentimental before it leaves the property. We are also happy to liaise with solicitors and executors to ease the administrative side.
What happens to the items you remove?

We sort belongings into what you keep, what can be donated, what may be worth selling, and what needs recycling or disposal. Usable furniture and household goods are directed to charity wherever possible, items of value are identified honestly thanks to our LAPADA antiques expertise, and the remainder is recycled or disposed of through proper channels to keep waste to a minimum. The aim is always to avoid sending perfectly usable things to landfill.
Can you combine a clearance with a removal or storage?

Absolutely — because we are a full removals and storage company as well as a clearance service, we can clear, pack, move and store all as one coordinated job. Items you wish to keep can go straight into our secure storage, including flexible short-term storage, then on to your new home when you are ready. It saves you arranging several different firms and keeps everything under one trusted roof.
